title: GemAttribute
description: Effect some attribute to new gem
body:
##  About
The GemsAttribute Mod adds some Attributes and Synergy to some new gems. When you wear them, their Synergy will produce very powerful effects, helping you better fight against monsters.

You can use Patchouli to get a guide book

It's open source, so you can refer to my code to help you learn some mod development.

##  Content
###  Attribute
- Amber:Reduces burning time
- Aquamarine:Add underwater movement efficiency
- CatsEyeStone:Add sneaking speed
- ConchPearl:Add swiming speed
- Helvite:Add movement speed
- Jade:Add Safe fall height
- Mahenge:Add Entity interaction distance
- Mica:Add knock back distance
- Partschinite:Add Armor value
- PhantomCrystal:Add explosion distance
- Ruby:Add attack damage
- Sapphire:Add attack speed
- SlimeCore:Add knock back
- Spinel:Add block interaction distance
- Topaz:Add extra oxygen consumption time
- Tsavorite:Add luck
### Synergy
- Absorb:Killing a creature grants 200% of its experience points
- BloodThirsty:Restores health equal to 50% of attack power when attacking
- Frenzy:Increases damage by 30%
- GuaranteedDamage:Each attack deals damage no less than 5% of the creature's maximum health
- HealthRecover:Gains Regeneration II for 5 seconds when health is below 30%
- ImmuneHarm:Immune to Poison, Wither, Slowness and Blindness
- Invisible:Gains Invisibility when crouching
- JumpResistance:Immune to fall damage
- NightVision:Grants Night Vision
- Perseverance:When taking fatal damage, deducts 10 experience levels and restores full health
- Resistance:Immune to 25% of incoming damage
- Slash:15% chance to instantly kill a creature when its health is below 45%
- TheLastGasp:Gains Strength II when health is below 25%
- WaterBreating:Grants Water Breathing
- Abhor:Inflicts Wither on the target when attacking

## Future
- Will add new gems
- Support 26.1 versions
- Add CurvingTable and New Mechanism
- Customized Bond Trigger Accessories
- Link Tinkers' Construct

## Issues
At present, no issues have been found. If you do come across any bugs, please submit an issue to my GitHub 

## FAQ
- Will there be a Fabric version?
- I don't think I will update the Fabric version because it would be very difficult for a novice like me to update both the loaders simultaneously.

- Will there be an update in Past Version?
- No,Because for Forge, I prefer Neoforge. I really dislike the cumbersome loading method of Forge.

## Credits&Licence
Thanks to z_bs for art and idea support

This mod adopts the LGPL license.
